title: Elon Musk's xAI sues Colorado over state's new AI law
description: xAI filed a lawsuit on Thursday seeking to block Colorado from enforcing a new law regulating artificial intelligence systems, escalating a fight over whether ​oversight should be handled by states or by Washington.
content:
April 9 (Reuters) - xAI filed a lawsuit on Thursday seeking to block Colorado from enforcing a new law regulating artificial intelligence systems, escalating a fight over whether ​oversight should be handled by states or by Washington.

title: Gen Z is using AI, but doesn’t feel great about it
description: More than half of Generation Zers living in the United States use generative artificial intelligence regularly, but their feelings about the technology are souring, according to a new survey.
content:
The survey of more than 1,500 people was conducted in February and March. Its results suggest that Americans’ animosity toward AI extends to a younger generation — one that is currently struggling to find its footing in the workplace.

title: Florida AG Uthmeier to probe OpenAI, ChatGPT role in FSU shooting
description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier announced plans Thursday to open investigations into the artificial intelligence platform OpenAI.
content:
April 9 (UPI) -- Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier announced plans Thursday to open investigations into the artificial intelligence platform OpenAI.

title: Environment Canada to use AI in new weather forecasting model
description: Environment and Climate Change Canada will use artificial intelligence to make its weather forecasts more accurate, the federal department said Thursday.
content:
Environment and Climate Change Canada will use artificial intelligence to make its weather forecasts more accurate, the federal department said Thursday.

description: OpenAI is planning to launch a cybersecurity model that rivals Anthropic's cybersecurity artificial intelligence model, Claude Mythos Preview.
content:

title: Universities of Wisconsin regents cite disputes over AI and other topics in president’s firing
description: Leaders on the board that oversees the Universities of Wisconsin rebuffed the fired system president’s claim that he was “blindsided” by their decision to oust him, telling lawmakers Thursday that he was slow to address pressing issues like artificial intelligence and feared upsetting policymakers, faculty and staff.
